title: AWS Solutions Architect Associate
description: Question batch for the Design Resilient Architectures domain.

questions.question: Which design best improves availability within one AWS Region?
questions.explanation: Multiple Availability Zones reduce the impact of a single-facility failure.

questions.question: Which service stores data with very high durability for backups?
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 is a durable service commonly used for backup storage.

questions.question: Which service can replace unhealthy instances automatically in a scaling group?
questions.explanation: EC2 Auto Scaling can launch replacements based on health checks and demand.

questions.question: Which RDS feature provides automatic failover to a standby in another AZ?
questions.explanation: RDS Multi-AZ provides a standby instance and automatic failover.

questions.question: Which S3 feature helps recover accidentally deleted objects?
questions.explanation: Versioning keeps previous object versions for recovery.

questions.question: Which service helps decouple components and absorb bursts of traffic?
questions.explanation: Amazon SQS buffers work between components and improves resilience.

questions.question: Which design best improves web app resilience against instance failure?
answers: Static IP on a single instance
questions.explanation: A load balancer across multiple instances reduces single-instance failure risk.

questions.question: Why are health checks important in resilient architectures?
answers: They help route traffic only to healthy resources
questions.explanation: Health checks support failover and traffic routing decisions.

questions.question: Which design is most appropriate for disaster recovery across geography?
questions.explanation: A second Region provides broader geographic resilience and DR options.

questions.question: Which messaging service supports fan-out resilience patterns?
questions.explanation: Amazon SNS can publish messages to multiple subscribers.

questions.question: Which database option helps scale reads without affecting the primary?
questions.explanation: Read Replicas offload read traffic and improve resilience under load.

questions.question: Which Route 53 capability can improve resilience during endpoint outages?
questions.explanation: Route 53 failover routing can redirect requests when a primary endpoint becomes unhealthy.

questions.question: Which architectural principle reduces tight coupling between services?
questions.explanation: Loose coupling makes systems more resilient to partial failures.

questions.question: Which practice best helps restore services after accidental data corruption?
questions.explanation: Reliable backups with tested recovery procedures improve resilience.

questions.question: Which storage option is ephemeral and should not be the only place for important data?
questions.explanation: Instance store data is temporary and tied to the instance lifecycle.

questions.question: Which service helps orchestrate event-driven workflows with retries?
questions.explanation: Step Functions supports workflow states, retries, and error handling.

questions.question: What is the main benefit of stateless application tiers?
answers: Instances can be replaced more easily
questions.explanation: Stateless tiers are easier to scale and replace during failures.

questions.question: Which architecture choice helps queue work when downstream systems slow down?
questions.explanation: Asynchronous messaging helps systems absorb delays and remain available.

questions.question: Which design best supports resilience for a stateful relational database?
questions.explanation: High availability plus backups improves resilience for stateful databases.

questions.question: Why is idempotency important in event-driven resilient systems?
answers: It lets retries avoid unwanted duplicate outcomes
questions.explanation: Idempotent operations allow safe retries after transient failures.

questions.question: Which pattern most directly reduces a single point of failure at the application tier?
answers: Run redundant instances behind a load balancer
questions.explanation: Redundancy behind a load balancer removes dependence on one instance.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of testing failover procedures regularly?
answers: To confirm recovery works as designed
questions.explanation: Resilience depends on verified recovery, not only theoretical design.

questions.question: Which design choice best limits the impact of a regional outage?
questions.explanation: A multi-Region strategy improves resilience against regional failures.

questions.question: Why are retries with backoff used in resilient distributed systems?
answers: To handle transient failures more gracefully
questions.explanation: Backoff helps reduce pressure while retrying transient failures.

questions.question: Which AWS storage choice best fits shared resilient Linux file access across instances?
questions.explanation: Amazon EFS provides resilient shared file storage for multiple instances.

questions.question: Which design best handles sudden workload spikes while keeping the app available?
questions.explanation: Elastic scaling helps applications remain available during demand spikes.

questions.question: Which service supports durable event ingestion from many producers?
questions.explanation: EventBridge provides managed event routing for event-driven architectures.

questions.question: Which practice most directly improves resilience of configuration management?
answers: Store infrastructure definitions in version control
Use ad hoc console changes only
questions.explanation: Versioned infrastructure definitions improve repeatability and recovery.

questions.question: Which design principle helps systems degrade gracefully during partial failures?
questions.explanation: Resilient architectures assume failures will happen and plan for them.

questions.question: Which feature best supports restoring S3 data after accidental overwrite?
questions.explanation: Versioning keeps older object versions for recovery after accidental changes.
